fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of document that you are about to create.

fields.name: Sell-to Customer No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the customer.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the document.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line number.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of entity that will be posted for this sales line, such as Item, Resource, or G/L Account.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a general ledger account, item, resource, additional cost, or fixed asset, depending on the contents of the Type field.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the location from where inventory items to the customer on the sales document are to be shipped by default.

fields.name: Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting group associated with the item. The program automatically retrieves the posting group from the Item Item table.

fields.name: Shipment Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies when items on the document are shipped or were shipped. A shipment date is usually calculated from a requested delivery date plus lead time.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the blanket sales order.

fields.name: Description 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies information in addition to the description.

fields.name: Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the item or resource's unit of measure, such as piece or hour.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the sales order line.

fields.name: Outstanding Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item are required on the sales line.

fields.name: Qty. to Invoice
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity that remains to be invoiced. It is calculated as Quantity - Qty. Invoiced.

fields.name: Qty. to Ship
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of items that remain to be shipped.

fields.name: Unit Price
fields.tooltip: Specifies the price of one unit of the item or resource. You can enter a price manually or have it entered according to the Price/Profit Calculation field on the related card.

fields.name: Unit Cost (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost, in LCY, of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: VAT %
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the VAT percentage that should be used.
The program automatically retrieves the VAT % from the General Posting Setup table when you fill in the Gen. Bus. Posting Group and Gen. Prod. Posting Group fields.
If the VAT Calculation Type field contains the Reverse Charge VAT option, the VAT % field will not be filled in. In this case, the line refers to business between your country/region and another EU-country/region, and VAT will not be calculated on the invoice. However, when you post the sales invoice the program creates a VAT entry with information about, reverse charge VAT and the VAT base (the amount used to calculate the VAT amount).

fields.name: Line Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the discount percentage that is granted for the item on the line.

fields.name: Line Discount Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the discount amount that is granted for the item on the line.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of amounts in the Line Amount field on the sales order lines.

fields.name: Amount Including VAT
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Allow Invoice Disc.
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the invoice line is included when the invoice discount is calculated.

fields.name: Gross Weight
fields.tooltip: Specifies the gross weight of one unit of the item. In the sales statistics window, the gross weight on the line is included in the total gross weight of all the lines for the particular sales document.

fields.name: Net Weight
fields.tooltip: Specifies the net weight of one unit of the item. In the sales statistics window, the net weight on the line is included in the total net weight of all the lines for the particular sales document.

fields.name: Units per Parcel
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of units per parcel of the item. In the sales statistics window, the number of units per parcel on the line helps to determine the total number of units for all the lines for the particular sales document.

fields.name: Unit Volume
fields.tooltip: Specifies the volume of one unit of the item. In the sales statistics window, the volume of one unit of the item on the line is included in the total volume of all the lines for the particular sales document.

fields.name: Appl.-to Item Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item ledger entry that the document or journal line is applied -to.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Customer Price Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the customer price group code. To see the price group codes that have been set up in the Customer Price Group table, click the field.
The program automatically retrieves the customer price group code from the Customer Price Group field on the sales header. The field is blank if no price group code has been entered on the sales header.
The program uses the code when entering the unit price for the item on the line. The program checks the Sales Price table to see whether the customers in the price group for the code should pay a different price from the one that has been entered in the Unit Price field on the item card.

fields.name: Job No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related project. If you fill in this field and the Project Task No. field, then a project ledger entry will be posted together with the sales line.

fields.name: Work Type Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the work type code of the service line linked to this entry.

fields.name: Outstanding Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the amount that still needs to be shipped before the order is filled.
The program calculates the amount as Amount Including VAT * Outstanding Quantity / Quantity.
The field is used only on sales order lines.
The program automatically updates this field.

fields.name: Qty. Shipped Not Invoiced
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how many units of the ordered item have been shipped but not yet invoiced.
The program updates the field when you post. It calculates the contents of the field as the difference between the Quantity Shipped and Quantity Invoiced fields.
You cannot delete a line that contains a quantity of items that have been shipped but not invoiced.

fields.name: Shipped Not Invoiced
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the amount of the ordered items that have been shipped but not yet invoiced.
The program updates the field when you post. It calculates the contents of the field as Amount Including VAT * Qty. Shipped Not Invoiced / Quantity.

fields.name: Quantity Shipped
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as shipped.

fields.name: Quantity Invoiced
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as invoiced.

fields.name: Shipment No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the shipment in the posted shipment list.
The program automatically copies the number from the posted shipment list.

fields.name: Shipment Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Shows the number the sales line has in the posted shipment list.
The program automatically copies the number from the posted shipment list.

fields.name: Profit %
fields.tooltip:
Specifies either the value from the Standard Profit % field on the card or zero.
The program automatically retrieves the profit percentage from the Item table.
This field is used only when you have chosen Item in the Type field.

fields.name: Bill-to Customer No.
fields.tooltip:
Shows the number of the customer who will receive the invoice for the items on the sales line.
The program automatically retrieves the bill-to customer number from the Bill-to Customer No. field on the sales header.

fields.name: Inv. Discount Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total calculated invoice discount amount for the line.

fields.name: Purchase Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the purchase order you have set up so that your vendor can ship the sales order directly (with a drop shipment) to your customer.

fields.name: Purch. Order Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line number on the purchase order you have set up so that your vendor can ship the sales order directly (with a drop shipment) to your customer.

fields.name: Drop Shipment
fields.tooltip: Specifies if your vendor ships the items directly to your customer.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's or customer's trade type to link transactions made for this business partner with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: VAT Calculation Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies which VAT calculation type will be used when this sales line is posted.
The program fills in the field automatically by using the VAT Bus. Posting Group and VAT Prod. Posting Group fields.
The VAT calculation type determines how the VAT will be calculated when this line is posted. There are three different VAT types and one type for use with sales tax:

Normal VAT

This option is used to calculate VAT for the amount on this line and to create a VAT ledger entry including the amount.


Reverse Charge VAT

This option is used when the line involves a sale to another EU country/region, where the seller does not collect VAT because the purchaser must compute and settle accounts with the tax authorities.


Full VAT

This option is used when the amount you are posting on this line consists entirely of VAT. This option may be useful if, for example, you make a corrective entry in order to correct a VAT calculation error.


Sales Tax

This option is used only if the program is set up to handle US sales tax. The option directs the program to retrieve information from specific sales tax tables and then calculate sales tax instead of VAT.

fields.name: Transaction Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the transaction type. To see the transaction type codes in the Transaction Type table, click the field.
The program automatically retrieves the transaction type code from the sales header. The field is blank if no code has been entered on the sales header.
You can change the contents of the field on individual sales lines.
The information is necessary for INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Transport Method
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the transport method code. To see the transaction type codes in the Transport Method table, click the field.
The program automatically retrieves the transport method from the sales header. The field is blank if no transport method has been entered on the sales header.
You can change the contents of the field on individual sales lines. The information is necessary for INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Attached to Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line number to which this sales line is attached.

fields.name: Exit Point
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code of the port of exit through which you ship the items out of your country/region.
The program retrieves the code automatically from the Exit Point field on the sales header.
The code is used for reporting to INTRASTAT and will appear on the Intrastat Journal.
To see the exit points that you can choose, click the field.

fields.name: Area
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the area of the customers address.
To see the area codes that have been set up, click the drop-down arrow to the right of the field.

Additional Information

The area code is copied from the sales header, but you can change the contents of the field on the individual sales lines.
The information is necessary for INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Transaction Specification
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the type of transaction specification used for the items in this line. To see the transaction specification codes that have been set up in the Transaction Specification table, click the field.
The program automatically retrieves the transaction specification code from the sales header. The field is blank if no code has been entered on the sales header.
You can change the contents of the field on individual sales lines. This information is necessary for INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Tax Category
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT category in connection with electronic document sending. For example, when you send sales documents through the PEPPOL service, the value in this field is used to populate several fields, such as the ClassifiedTaxCategory element in the Item group. It is also used to populate the TaxCategory element in both the TaxSubtotal and AllowanceCharge group. The number is based on the UNCL5305 standard.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax area code for the customer.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip: Specifies if this vendor charges you sales tax for purchases.

fields.name: Tax Group Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax group code for the tax detail entry.

fields.name: VAT Clause Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the VAT Clause Code that is associated with the sales line. The value in this field is based on the value that is associated with the information that is specified in the VAT Posting Setup.

Additional Information

If you change the VAT Prod. Posting Group or the VAT Bus. Posting Group on the sales line, the information in the VAT Clause Code field is updated to match.
After the sales document is posted, the VAT Clause Code is copied to other sales documents, such as the sales invoice and sales credit memo. In turn, when a report is created from these documents, the VAT clause description text is displayed on the report.
If you do not define a VAT clause for a line on a sales document, the VAT clause section of the report is not created.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's VAT specification to link transactions made for this vendor with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: VAT Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT product posting group. Links business transactions made for the item, resource, or G/L account with the general ledger, to account for VAT amounts resulting from trade with that record.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the currency code for the amount on this line.

fields.name: Outstanding Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the amount for the items on the order that still needs to be shipped before the order is filled in LCY.
The program calculates the contents of this field by using the amount in the Outstanding Amount field and the relevant exchange rate.
The program automatically updates this field.

fields.name: Shipped Not Invoiced (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the amount for the ordered items that have been shipped but not yet invoiced in LCY.
The program updates the field when you post. It calculates the contents of this field by using the amount in the Shipped Not Invoiced field and the relevant exchange rate.

fields.name: Reserved Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been reserved.

fields.name: Reserve
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether items will never, automatically (Always), or optionally be reserved for this customer. Optional means that you must manually reserve items for this customer.

fields.name: Blanket Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the blanket order that the record originates from.

fields.name: Blanket Order Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the blanket order line that the record originates from.

fields.name: VAT Base Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total net amount of this sales line (excluding VAT).

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: System-Created Entry
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Line Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the net amount, excluding any invoice discount amount, that must be paid for products on the line.

fields.name: VAT Difference
fields.tooltip:
Shows the difference between the VAT amount that is automatically calculated and the VAT amount that you entered manually in the VAT Amount field.
The VAT difference displayed here is distributed proportionally between purchase lines with the same VAT identifier.

fields.name: Inv. Disc. Amount to Invoice
fields.tooltip: Specifies the actual invoice discount amount that will be posted for the line in next invoice.

fields.name: VAT Identifier
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Copies the contents from the VAT Identifier field in the VAT Posting Setup window.

fields.name: IC Partner Ref. Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item or account in your IC partner's company that corresponds to the item or account on the line.

fields.name: IC Partner Reference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the IC partner. If the line is being sent to one of your intercompany partners, this field is used together with the IC Partner Ref. Type field to indicate the item or account in your partner's company that corresponds to the line.

fields.name: Prepayment %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment percentage to use to calculate the prepayment for sales.

fields.name: Prepmt. Line Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment amount of the line in the currency of the sales document if a prepayment percentage is specified for the sales line.

fields.name: Prepmt. Amt. Inv.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment amount that has already been invoiced to the customer for this sales line.

fields.name: Prepmt. Amt. Incl. VAT
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Prepayment Amount
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Prepmt. VAT Base Amt.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total prepayment amount for this sales line (excluding VAT).

fields.name: Prepayment VAT %
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the VAT percentage that should be used for prepayment invoices.
The program automatically retrieves the VAT % from the General Posting Setup table when you fill in the Gen. Bus. Posting Group and Gen. Prod. Posting Group fields.
If the VAT Calculation Type field contains the Reverse Charge VAT option, the VAT % field will not be filled in. In this case, the line refers to business between your country/region and another EU-country/region, and VAT will not be calculated on the prepayment invoice. However, when you post the prepayment invoice, the program creates a VAT entry with information about reverse charge VAT and the VAT base (the amount used to calculate the VAT amount).

fields.name: Prepmt. VAT Calc. Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies which VAT calculation type will be used when a prepayment invoice for this sales line is posted.
The program fills in the field automatically by using the VAT Bus. Posting Group and VAT Prod. Posting Group fields.
The VAT calculation type determines how the VAT will be calculated when the prepayment invoice is posted. There are three different VAT types and one type for use with sales tax:
Normal VAT
This option is used to calculate VAT for the amount on this line and to create a VAT ledger entry including the amount.
Reverse Charge VAT
This option is used when the line involves a sale to another EU country/region, where the seller does not collect VAT because the purchaser must compute and settle accounts with the tax authorities.
Full VAT
This option cannot be used for prepayment.
Sales Tax
This option is used only if the program is set up to handle US sales tax. The option directs the program to retrieve information from specific sales tax tables and then calculate sales tax instead of VAT.

fields.name: Prepayment VAT Identifier
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Automatically copies the value from the VAT Identifier field in the VAT Posting Setup window.

fields.name: Prepayment Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the tax area code of the customer.
The program automatically retrieves the code from the Tax Area Code field on the sales header.
The program will use the tax area code, in combination with the tax group code on the sales line, to determine the sales tax % and the G/L accounts for sales tax posting in connection with prepayments.
Normally, you should not change the content of this field, but it may be necessary in special cases where the default code is not appropriate.

fields.name: Prepayment Tax Liable
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether sales to the customer on the sales header are liable to sales tax.
The program automatically retrieves the code from the Tax Liable field on the sales header.

fields.name: Prepayment Tax Group Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the tax group code of the item on this line.
The program automatically retrieves the code from the Tax Group Code field on the item card when the No. field on this sales line is filled in.
The program will use the tax group code, in combination with the tax area code on the sales line, to determine the sales tax % and the G/L accounts for sales tax posting in connection with prepayments.
Normally, you should not change the content of this field, but it may be necessary in special cases where the default code is not appropriate.

fields.name: Prepmt Amt to Deduct
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment amount that has already been deducted from ordinary invoices posted for this sales order line.

fields.name: Prepmt Amt Deducted
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment amount that has already been deducted from ordinary invoices posted for this sales order line.

fields.name: Prepayment Line
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Prepmt. Amount Inv. Incl. VAT
fields.tooltip:
Shows the prepayment amount including VAT that has already been invoiced to the customer for this sales line. The amount is displayed in the currency of the sales document.
You cannot change the content of this field. If you want to void the prepayment, you must post a Prepayment Credit Memo for the sales line.

fields.name: Prepmt. Amount Inv. (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the prepayment amount that has already been invoiced to the customer for this sales line. The amount is displayed in the local currency of the sales document.
If you have placed a check mark in the Prices Including VAT field before posting the prepayment invoice, the amount displayed will include VAT.
You cannot change the content of this field. If you want to void the prepayment, you must post a prepayment credit memo for the sales line.

fields.name: IC Partner Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the intercompany partner that the transaction is related to if the entry was created from an intercompany transaction.

fields.name: Prepmt. VAT Amount Inv. (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the VAT amount that has already been invoiced to the customer for this sales line.
The amount is displayed in the currency of the sales document.

fields.name: Prepayment VAT Difference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the difference between the prepayment VAT amount that is automatically calculated and the prepayment VAT amount that you entered manually in the prepayment VAT amount field.

fields.name: Prepmt VAT Diff. to Deduct
fields.tooltip: Shows the prepayment VAT difference that will be deducted from the next ordinary invoice for this line in the currency of the sales order.

fields.name: Prepmt VAT Diff. Deducted
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment VAT difference that has already been deducted from ordinary invoices posted for this sales order line in the currency of the sales order.

fields.name: IC Item Reference No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the IC item reference. If the line is being sent to one of your intercompany partners, this field is used together with the IC Partner Ref. Type field to indicate the item or account in your partner's company that corresponds to the line.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Qty. to Assemble to Order
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the blanket sales line quantity that you want to supply by assembly.

fields.name: Qty. to Asm. to Order (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how many units of the sales line quantity that you want to supply by assembly. The units are expressed in the base unit of measure.
If the assembly item on the sales order line is set up to be assembled to order, then a linked assembly order header is automatically created, and the value in this field reflects the value in the Quantity (Base) field on that linked assembly order header.
You define an items assembly process in the Assembly Policy field on the item card of the assembly item.

fields.name: ATO Whse. Outstanding Qty.
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many assemble-to-order units on the sales order line need to be assembled and handled in warehouse documents.

fields.name: ATO Whse. Outstd. Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many assemble-to-order units on the sales order line remain to be assembled and handled in warehouse documents.

fields.name: Job Task No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related project task.

fields.name: Job Contract Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the entry number of the project planning line that the sales line is linked to.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date when the posting of the sales document will be recorded. By default, todays date is entered.
The posting date will be copied to all general, customer, and item ledger entries when posting.

fields.name: Deferral Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the deferral template that governs how revenue earned with this sales document is deferred to the different accounting periods when the good or service was delivered.

fields.name: Returns Deferral Start Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting date of the returns deferral period.

fields.name: Selected Alloc. Account No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the allocation account number that will be used to distribute the amounts during the posting process.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the variant of the item on the line.

fields.name: Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin where the items are picked or put away.

fields.name: Qty. per Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies an auto-filled number if you have included Sales Unit of Measure on the item card and a quantity in the Qty. per Unit of Measure field.

fields.name: Planned
fields.tooltip: Contains Yes if the sales line is included in production planning.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit of measure that is used to determine the value in the Unit Price field on the sales line.

fields.name: Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The quantity in the field is the Quantity on the line expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Outstanding Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the outstanding quantity expressed in the base units of measure.

fields.name: Qty. to Invoice (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The quantity in the field is the Qty. to Invoice on the line expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Qty. to Ship (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The quantity in the field is the Qty. to Ship on the line expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Qty. Shipped Not Invd. (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The quantity in the field is the Qty. Shipped Not Invoiced on the line expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Qty. Shipped (Base)
fields.tooltip:
This field is automatically updated. The quantity in the field is the Quantity Shipped on the line expressed in base units of measure.
The program updates the field when you post.

fields.name: Qty. Invoiced (Base)
fields.tooltip:
This field is automatically updated. The quantity in the field is the Quantity Invoiced on the line expressed in base units of measure.
The program updates the field when you post.

fields.name: Reserved Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reserved quantity of the item expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: FA Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that will be used on related fixed asset ledger entries.

fields.name: Depreciation Book Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the depreciation book to which the line will be posted if you have selected Fixed Asset in the Type field for this line.

fields.name: Depr. until FA Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies if depreciation was calculated until the FA posting date of the line.

fields.name: Duplicate in Depreciation Book
fields.tooltip: Specifies a depreciation book code if you want the journal line to be posted to that depreciation book, as well as to the depreciation book in the Depreciation Book Code field.

fields.name: Use Duplication List
fields.tooltip: Specifies, if the type is Fixed Asset, that information on the line is to be posted to all the assets defined depreciation books.

fields.name: Responsibility Center
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the customer's responsibility center code. To see the responsibility center codes that have been set up in the Responsibility Center table, click the field.
The program automatically retrieves the responsibility center code from the Responsibility Center Code field on the sales header. The field is blank if no responsibility center code has been entered on the sales header.

fields.name: Out-of-Stock Substitution
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. You cannot change or delete the contents of this field. If an item is out of stock, the program automatically enters a check mark in this field.

fields.name: Substitution Available
fields.tooltip: Specifies that a substitute is available for the item on the sales line.

fields.name: Originally Ordered No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item that was originally ordered.

fields.name: Originally Ordered Var. Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the variant that was originally ordered.

fields.name: Item Category Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the item category code for the item.
You can use item categories to group default item setup values to make creating new items easier. An item category code specifies the following values:

Default general product posting group.


Default inventory posting group.


Default VAT product posting group.


Default costing method.


To see or select item categories in the Item Category window, click the drop-down arrow in the field.

fields.name: Nonstock
fields.tooltip: Specifies that this item is a catalog item.

fields.name: Purchasing Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for a special procurement method, such as drop shipment.

fields.name: Special Order
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the item on the sales line is a special-order item.

fields.name: Special Order Purchase No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the purchase order for a special order. (This order is shipped to your warehouse for a specific customer.)
The program automatically fills in this field, if the code you entered for this line was the code for a special order.

fields.name: Special Order Purch. Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the line that contains the special order.
The program automatically fills in this field, if the code you entered for this line was the code for a special order.

fields.name: Item Reference No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the referenced item number. If you enter a cross reference between yours and your vendor's or customer's item number, then this number will override the standard item number when you enter the reference number on a sales or purchase document.

fields.name: Whse. Outstanding Qty.
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units on the sales order line remain to be handled in warehouse documents.

fields.name: Whse. Outstanding Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units on the sales order line remain to be handled in warehouse documents.

fields.name: Completely Shipped
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether you have shipped all the items on the line.

fields.name: Requested Delivery Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that the customer has asked for the order to be delivered.

fields.name: Promised Delivery Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that you have promised to deliver the order, as a result of the Order Promising function.

fields.name: Shipping Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies how long it takes from when the items are shipped from the warehouse to when they are delivered.

fields.name: Outbound Whse. Handling Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies a date formula for the time it takes to get items ready to ship from this location. The time element is used in the calculation of the delivery date as follows: Shipment Date + Outbound Warehouse Handling Time = Planned Shipment Date + Shipping Time = Planned Delivery Date.

fields.name: Planned Delivery Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the planned date that the shipment will be delivered at the customer's address. If the customer requests a delivery date, the program calculates whether the items will be available for delivery on this date. If the items are available, the planned delivery date will be the same as the requested delivery date. If not, the program calculates the date that the items are available for delivery and enters this date in the Planned Delivery Date field.

fields.name: Planned Shipment Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that the shipment should ship from the warehouse. If the customer requests a delivery date, the program calculates the planned shipment date by subtracting the shipping time from the requested delivery date. If the customer does not request a delivery date or the requested delivery date cannot be met, the program calculates the content of this field by adding the shipment time to the shipping date.

fields.name: Shipping Agent Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the shipping agent who is transporting the items.

fields.name: Shipping Agent Service Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the service, such as a one-day delivery, that is offered by the shipping agent.

fields.name: Allow Item Charge Assignment
fields.tooltip: Specifies that you can assign item charges to this line.

fields.name: Qty. to Assign
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item charge are assigned to the line originally.

fields.name: Qty. Assigned
fields.tooltip: Specifies how much of the item charge has been assigned.

fields.name: Return Qty. to Receive
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of items that remain to be shipped.

fields.name: Return Qty. to Receive (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The quantity in the field is the Return Qty. to Receive on the line expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Return Qty. Rcd. Not Invd.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity of ordered items that have been returned but not yet invoiced.
The program updates the field when you post. It calculates the contents of the field as the difference between the Return Qty. Received and Quantity Invoiced fields.
You cannot delete a line that contains a quantity of returned items that have been received but not invoiced.

fields.name: Ret. Qty. Rcd. Not Invd.(Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The quantity in the field is the Return Qty. Rcd. Not Invd. on the line expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Return Rcd. Not Invd.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the value of the items that you have received as returned from the customer. The items have been returned, but they are not yet posted as invoiced.
The amount is in the currency of the sales document.

fields.name: Return Rcd. Not Invd. (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the value in LCY of the items returned from the customer. Because the items have been returned, they were not posted as invoiced.

fields.name: Return Qty. Received
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as shipped.

fields.name: Return Qty. Received (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of items that you have posted as received because they have been returned from the customer.
The quantity is expressed in the base unit of measure.

fields.name: Appl.-from Item Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item ledger entry that the document or journal line is applied from.

fields.name: Item Charge Qty. to Handle
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many items the item charge will be assigned to on the line. It can be either equal to Qty. to Assign or to zero. If it is zero, the item charge will not be assigned to the line.

fields.name: BOM Item No.
fields.tooltip:
Contains the item number of the exploded BOM item.
The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Return Receipt No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the return receipt that corresponds to the sales return order.

fields.name: Return Receipt Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the return receipt line that corresponds to the sales return order line.

fields.name: Return Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code explaining why the item was returned.

fields.name: Allow Line Disc.
fields.tooltip: Specifies that a line discount is calculated when the price is offered.

fields.name: Customer Disc. Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the customer discount group code. To see the discount group codes that have been set up in the Customer Discount Group table, click the field.
The program automatically retrieves the customer discount group code from the Customer Disc. Group field on the sales header. The field is blank if no discount group code has been entered on the sales header.
The program uses the code when entering the unit price for the item on the line. The program checks the Sales Price table to see whether the customers in the price group for the code should pay a different price from the one that has been entered in the Unit Price field on the item card.

fields.name: Attached Doc Count
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of attachments.

fields.name: Attached Lines Count
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of non-inventory product lines attached to the sales line.

fields.name: Sell-to Customer Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the customer.
